lowQualityBlocks.excerpt: One of the newest Helios-class ships, fully powered by LNG. Features the Ocean Deck with infinity pool and the Fun Park with a rope garden.
The world
Twin sister to AIDAprima, featuring the covered Beach Club for year-round summer vibes and the Four Elements activity deck.
The first Hyperion-class ship, known for its foil-roofed Beach Club, Racer water slide, and extensive organic dining options.
The final Sphinx-class ship, featuring a Nordic-inspired spa design and the popular Brauhaus (brewery) at sea.
Sphinx-class favorite with the onboard brewery, floating restaurant
Features a massive Wellness Oasis and distinct Mediterranean design elements throughout the ship.
The first AIDA ship to introduce the onboard brewery, serving fresh beer brewed right on the ocean.
Offers the unique Moonlight Bar and a giant LED screen on the pool deck for open-air movie nights.
Known for its bell-shaped Theatrium glass walls and the 4D Cinemare experience.
The first of the Sphinx class, revolutionizing the fleet with the open Theatrium concept instead of a traditional theater. Refreshed in 2025
Fleet of modern river cruise ships operating on European rivers including Rhine, Danube, and Douro.
Modern river cruise ship with eco-friendly technology.
Largest ship in the A-ROSA river fleet, featuring hybrid propulsion.
First cruise line dedicated to the Arabian market. Formerly World Dream, fully refurbished before entering service in December 2024.
The first ship in the fleet powered by LNG and featuring a roller coaster attraction at sea. This vessel introduces multiple themed zones an
Sister ship to Mardi Gras, introduced in 2022. Features multiple themed zones and entertainment areas similar to the Excel class design.
The third Excel-class ship, launched in 2023. Features multiple themed zones and entertainment areas, homeported in Galveston.
Originally built for Costa, now part of the Carnival fleet. Features Italian-themed decor and dining options.
Joining the fleet in 2024 after a 42-day refit and rebranding at Navantia shipyard in Cádiz, Spain. Brings Italian style to the West Coast w
Introduced an elevated track ride attraction at sea and features an IMAX theater for movies.
Features a large water park, family-friendly venues, and multiple entertainment zones.
Features an indoor trampoline park and extensive water park areas for guests.
Completed a month-long dry dock in March 2025, introducing various upgrades and enhancements to public spaces and amenities.
Extensively renovated in 2021. Previously operated under a different name. Features updated dining and entertainment venues.
Extensively renovated in 2013. Previously operated under a different name. Features an adults-only relaxation area spanning multiple decks.
Transformed in 2019 from Carnival Triumph.
Joined the fleet in 2022. Features Italian-themed decor and design elements.
Introduced the revolutionary Magic Carpet and Infinite Verandas.
Scheduled for extensive dry dock renovation in early 2026, introducing new amenities, reinvented outdoor spaces, and ship-wide stateroom and
Purpose-built for the Galapagos Islands.
Mid-sized ship specializing in Greek Islands and Eastern Mediterranean cruises.
Formerly AIDAaura, now sailing Greek Islands and Mediterranean routes.
The only true ocean liner currently in service.
The first Triton Class ship, featuring the AquaMouse and Frozen dining.
Adventure-themed sister to the Wish, debuting late 2024.
Acquired from Genting, to be based in Singapore.
First ship in the Explora Journeys fleet, offering luxury ocean travel with sustainable technology.
Second ship in the Explora Journeys fleet, featuring innovative design and luxury amenities.
Formerly Amsterdam, now sailing for Fred. Olsen with traditional British style.
Formerly Rotterdam, offering intimate cruising with British hospitality.
Smaller ship offering traditional British cruising experience.
Plug-in hybrid cruise ship with advanced battery systems, allowing up to four hours of emissions-free cruising.
Sister ship to Havila Polaris, featuring sustainable technology for Norwegian coastal voyages.
Formerly Celebrity Galaxy, extensively refurbished for the UK market.
Formerly Splendour of the Seas, now sailing for TUI UK.
Formerly Celebrity Century, refurbished for Marella Cruises.
Formerly Grandeur of the Seas, now part of the Marella fleet.
The first LNG-powered ship in the MSC fleet.
Scheduled for substantial dry dock refurbishment before summer 2026, including retrofitting the Yacht Club suite enclave.
Fleet of river cruise ships operating on European rivers with German-speaking service.
Small expedition ship for adventure cruises.
First of the Prima Class, offering the most outdoor deck space of any new cruise ship.
Sister to Prima, featuring the Indulge Food Hall and The Drop slide.
Debuting in 2025 as a next-generation Prima Class ship, introducing new outdoor attractions and expanded public spaces.
Features the largest race track at sea and the Galaxy Pavilion.
Designed for Alaska cruising with a massive observation lounge. Underwent significant refurbishment in early 2025 with upgrades to public ar
Originally built for China, renovated in 2019 and again in 2024.
Revolutionary design with curved staterooms and The Haven.
The only major US-flagged cruise ship, sailing exclusively in Hawaii.
Completely modernized in a $100 million renovation in 2020.
Formally named in Miami on November 13, 2025, launched its inaugural season in the Caribbean.
Formerly Royal Princess, now offering German-language cruises.
Classic ship offering traditional German cruising experience.
German river cruise operator with ships on Rhine, Danube, and Elbe rivers.
Formerly Golden Princess, now sailing Australian and South Pacific routes.
Formerly Star Princess, offering cruises from Australia and New Zealand.
The first Sphere Class ship, featuring the Dome and powered by LNG.
A large cruise ship launched in 2024. Features multiple pools, water park areas, and an entertainment dome.
A large cruise ship scheduled to launch in 2025, similar in design to Icon of the Seas.
Entered service in 2024, offering short cruises from Florida to destinations like Perfect Day at CocoCay and Nassau, Bahamas.
A large cruise ship launched in 2022. Features multiple themed neighborhoods including dedicated suite areas.
Features a multi-deck slide, laser tag arena, and Broadway-style musical entertainment.
A large cruise ship that introduced water slides to this ship class and features a robotic bar.
A large cruise ship, originally launched in 2010 and significantly renovated in 2025. Features new dining venues, updated staterooms, and en
A large cruise ship, originally launched in 2009 and extensively renovated in 2024.
A large cruise ship featuring an indoor sports complex and an elevated observation capsule.
Designed for the Asian market with enhanced suite accommodations and dedicated areas for suite guests.
Renovated in 2019 with new water slide attractions.
All-balcony ship designed for the over-50s market, featuring single cabins and traditional British style.
Sister ship to Spirit of Discovery, offering all-balcony accommodations and British-inspired dining.
A modern interpretation of the classic tall ship design, combining traditional sailing with contemporary luxury and comfort.
The newest addition to the fleet, featuring state-of-the-art technology while maintaining the tradition of authentic tall ship sailing. Eco-
Sister ship to Sea Cloud Spirit, continuing the legacy of luxury tall ship cruising with modern amenities and sustainable technology.
Italian ferry operator offering Mediterranean crossings and short cruises between Italy and surrounding islands.
The first Virgin Voyages ship, focused on adults-only sailings with modern design and nightlife-forward entertainment.
